name: BLE Component
description: The `esp32_ble` component in ESPHome sets up the Bluetooth LE stack on the device so that a [Esp32 Ble Server](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_server/) can run.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the BLE device.

config_entries.label: Advertising
config_entries.description: Manually enable BLE advertising support. This is automatically enabled when using [Esp32 Ble Server](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_server/) or [Esp32 Ble Beacon](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_beacon/). Only set this to `true` if you need advertising functionality without those components. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Advertising Cycle Time
config_entries.description: The time interval for cycling through multiple advertisements. Only applicable when advertising is enabled. Defaults to `10s`.

config_entries.label: Auth Req Mode
config_entries.description: The authentication request mode. Defaults to the ESP-IDF default. Leave unspecified unless your device needs special handling.

options.label: Secure connections with MITM and bonding

config_entries.label: Connection Timeout
config_entries.description: The maximum time to wait for a BLE connection to be established. Defaults to `20s`.

config_entries.label: Disable Bt Logs
config_entries.description: When enabled, disables Bluetooth logging categories that are not used by the configured components. This saves flash memory by only including the loggers needed by your configuration. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Enable On Boot
config_entries.description: If enabled, the BLE interface will be enabled on boot. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: IO Capability
config_entries.description: The IO capability of this ESP32, used for securely connecting to other BLE devices. Defaults to `none`.

options.label: No IO capability (Connections that require PIN code authentication will fail)
Only a keyboard to enter PIN codes (or a fixed PIN code)
Only a display to show PIN codes
A display to show PIN codes and buttons to confirm or deny the connection

config_entries.label: Max Connections
config_entries.description: The maximum number of simultaneous BLE connections (client + server combined). Defaults to `3`.

config_entries.label: Max Key Size
config_entries.description: Maximum encryption key size to support. Must be between `7` and `16`. Leave unspecified to use the ESP32 default unless your device needs special handling.

config_entries.label: Max Notifications
config_entries.description: The maximum number of BLE characteristics that can have notifications enabled across all connections. Defaults to `12`.

config_entries.label: Min Key Size
config_entries.description: Minimum encryption key size requirement from peer. Must be between `7` and `16`. Leave unspecified to use the ESP32 default unless your device needs special handling.

config_entries.label: Use PSRAM
config_entries.description: Requests that the Bluetooth stack allocate its memory buffers from PSRAM instead of internal RAM, freeing approximately 40 kB for other uses. Defaults to `false`. Requires PSRAM to be configured.
